what_it_does: This file is the prescriptive module constitution for company/introspection; it defines the platform-agnostic Mirror-Registry engine, the four-verb DISCOVER/CLASSIFY/PROJECT/REFRESH circuit, the closed classification rules R1–R5 plus the R6 swap-kind exclusion, the adapter registration pattern, and the invariants that keep platform-specific strings out of Level-1 code.
observations: The engine runs a four-verb circuit: DISCOVER, CLASSIFY, PROJECT, REFRESH.
Classification uses five closed rules R1–R5 with explicit priority R1 > R2 > R3 > R5 > R4.
R6 is not a classify rule; it is a swap-kind head-default exclusion applied before R1 sees the transport-invariant set.
Level-1 code in engine.py, rules.py, and adapters/ must contain zero platform-name literals; tests/introspection_acceptance.py greps for banned strings and fails the build on any hit.
transport_invariants is derived live from the consumer's spawn head via a head_builder thunk, never hand-typed.
CapabilityRegistry is a module-level singleton with set_capability_registry, deliberately diverging from the fresh-discover-on-each-call sibling registries because binary discovery is expensive.
Discovery is deferred; Suite.__init__ installs an empty registry and cap:// stays fail-loud until a LEAD run or stub populates it.
A three-platform generalization proof is claimed: claude-code (Commander.js), gh_cli (Cobra), and codex-cli (clap) all registered as pure data rows with zero Level-1 edits.
A recorded adapter gap notes that clap prints descriptions on the next line, so cli-help captures empty descriptions for codex-cli flags.
Store writes for version stamps and introspection cache use direct open(), not FsStore CAS.
PlatformEntry loads via Pydantic model_validate so novel Literal values fail loud at load time.
The file lists several pending LEAD live-verify items marked yellow.
standouts: The build-failing grep that bans platform-name literals from Level-1 engine/rules/adapter code.
The three-platform generalization proof across Commander.js, Cobra, and clap with no Level-1 edits.
The R6 swap-kind head-default exclusion that prevents operator-supplied head defaults from being mis-classified as transport locks.
The deliberate singleton divergence of CapabilityRegistry, justified by the cost of spawning a binary on every cap:// resolution.
The posture-is-derived, registry-is-truth principle: no hand-stored posture column remains.
conventions: Constitution format with YAML front matter, type=constitution, register=prescriptive.
Fail-loud / no-silent-failures for missing adapters, empty discovery, sub-floor parse, empty R1 set, and unclassified novel flags.
Closed maps for DISCOVERERS and INVOKERS; adding a kind means adding a row and building the adapter.
Derive-don't-hand-type: transport_invariants, supervisor posture, and classification all come from data plus rules.
Direct open() for flat operational store files; FsStore CAS reserved for content-addressed nodes.
Pydantic model_validate for deeply nested PlatformEntry sub-models.
Deferred expensive discovery to avoid spawning a CLI binary at Suite initialization.
Acceptance tests serve as architecture gates: leak grep, drift gate, crosscheck, genproof.
concerns: Multiple LEAD live-verify items are still marked yellow, so some claims in the file remain unvalidated here.
The clap description-line adapter gap is recorded but not yet resolved.
Multi-platform cap:// namespacing is noted as a required next step but not built.
The constitution references many external ticket IDs (F-FIX-*, PG-*, C-*) whose definitions live outside this file, creating maintenance risk.
The file mixes prescriptive architecture with current build/verification status, so status sections may stale quickly.
notes: A dense constitution that treats the introspection engine as a data-driven classifier whose platform-agnosticism is enforced by build-failing grep tests and proven by adding three distinct CLI platforms as pure data rows.
questions: Which exact eight Level-1 files are included in the banned-string grep?
What is the precise shape of SignalSets and how is it populated for each platform row?
How does the curator gate and inbox.surface implementation handle the R4 novelty reject default?
What is the relationship between PlatformRegistry and CapabilityRegistry at load time versus discovery time?
How is the R6 exclusion applied when a flag belongs to multiple capability_axes?
Where are the acceptance tests located and how are they invoked relative to this constitution?
purpose_vs_actual: matches — the file states it is a prescriptive constitution for the introspection module and then lays out exactly those rules, invariants, and verification claims.
apparent_themes: Derive invariants from consumer behavior rather than hand-authoring them
Closed rule sets with explicit priority and scope guards
Generalization by adding rows, not editing engine code
intention_signals: The file is trying to make platform support a configuration problem rather than a code-change problem, while guaranteeing that any loss of invariants (leaked platform names, missing adapters, stale registries, empty rule inputs) fails loudly instead of silently degrading posture classification.
novelty: The banned-platform-literal grep that fails the build, the R6 swap-kind head-default exclusion that prevents operator-supplied defaults from being mis-locked, and the three-platform generalization proof (Commander.js, Cobra, clap) with zero edits to Level-1 engine code.
contribution: It contributes the governing contract for the introspection module: the four-verb engine semantics, the closed classification rule set, the platform-agnosticism invariants and their enforcement mechanism, the registry-load patterns, and the recorded verification status for generalization and wiring.
summary_for_embedding: The introspection module constitution prescribing a platform-agnostic Mirror-Registry engine with a four-verb circuit, closed posture rules, derived transport invariants, and a build-failing ban on platform-name literals in Level-1 code.
intention_for_embedding: To enforce a data-driven, fail-loud boundary between platform-agnostic discovery/classification engine code and platform-specific registry rows so that new platforms can be added without modifying the engine.
